countries.title: Popular Destinations
countries.description: Discover the best countries for your international education journey with our expert guidance.

russia.name: Russia
russia.slogan: A world of boundless horizons and fundamental knowledge
russia.description: With a rich history of scientific excellence, Russia offers world-class education in medicine, engineering, and the arts at an affordable cost.

china.name: China
china.slogan: Synergy of ancient wisdom and future technologies
china.description: Experience the pulse of the world's fastest-growing economy while studying at prestigious universities leading in tech and innovation.

turkey.name: Turkey
turkey.slogan: Crossroads of civilizations and vibrant student life
turkey.description: Enjoy a unique blend of Eastern and Western cultures, modern campuses, and a welcoming atmosphere for international students.

cyprus.name: Cyprus
cyprus.slogan: A sunny island of European education and comfort
cyprus.description: Safe, sunny, and strictly following European standards, Cyprus is an ideal location for students seeking quality and relaxation.

belarus.name: Belarus
belarus.description: Known for its high academic standards and safe environment, Belarus provides excellent value and traditional European schooling.

bulgaria.name: Bulgaria
bulgaria.slogan: Affordable European education on the Black Sea coast
bulgaria.description: Gateway to the European Union with reputable medical and artistic programs in a beautiful Black Sea setting.

destinations.title: Explore Destinations
destinations.description: Explore our most popular study destinations and find the perfect city for your academic journey.

cities.name: Moscow
cities.description: Russia's vibrant capital, where history and modern innovation collide. Home to world-class medical and engineering universities.

cities.name: St. Petersburg
cities.description: The cultural heart of Russia. Known for its breathtaking architecture and prestigious arts and humanities programs.

cities.name: Beijing
cities.description: The center of China's political and educational world. A top destination for technology and business studies.

cities.name: Shanghai
cities.description: A global financial hub and future-looking metropolis. Perfect for students pursuing economics and international finance.

cities.name: Istanbul
cities.description: A bridge between continents with a rich academic heritage. Renowned for its history, architecture, and engineering faculties.

cities.name: Ankara
cities.description: Turkey's administrative center and home to top-tier state universities specializing in law, politics, and social sciences.

q1.q: 1. What is the minimum passport validity required for admission?
q1.a: Most countries require the passport to be valid for at least 12–18 months at the time of application or entry.

q2.q: 2. Can I get a certificate to replace my passport?
q2.a: Yes, an Acceptance Letter from the university serves as a basis for passport replacement or extension.

q3.q: 3. What is the minimum age for admission? Are there age limits?
q3.a: Usually, admission starts from 17–18 years old. There is typically no upper age limit for paid education, but scholarships may have limits (25–30 years).

q4.q: 4. What documents are needed for contract-based admission?
q4.a: Standard package: passport, photo, high school diploma/degree with translation. Sometimes a medical certificate and bank statement are required.

q5.q: 5. What documents are needed for scholarship admission?
q5.a: Passport, photo, translated educational documents, motivation letter, recommendation letters, certificates (IELTS/TOEFL/HSK), portfolio, and good academic standing.

q6.a: Depends on the country. Fall intake generally runs from February to July, Spring intake from September to November. Scholarship deadlines are often earlier (winter).

q7.q: 7. How much money is needed to start?
q7.a: Initial expenses (visa, tickets, initial fees) usually range from $500 to $1500, depending on the country.

q8.a: Yes, many countries (Turkey, Germany, Czech Republic, etc.) require the recognition (nostrification) procedure for foreign educational documents.

q9.a: Yes, English-taught programs are available in most major universities worldwide, but the choice of majors might be limited compared to the national language.

q10.q: 10. What is the approximate cost of education and living?
q10.a: Education: from $1500 to $8000+ per year. Living: from $300 (China, Turkey) to $800+ (Canada, EU) per month.

q11.q: 11. Are there free programs or scholarships?
q11.a: Yes, there are government grants (CSC, Türkiye Bursları, Russian grants) and university scholarships covering from 25% to 100% of the cost.

q12.q: 12. How do I get a student visa and how long does it take?
q12.a: You will need a university invitation, passport, finances, and insurance. The processing time varies from 2 weeks to 3 months.

q13.a: In most countries (Russia, Turkey, Germany, Canada), students are allowed to work up to 20 hours per week during studies and full-time during holidays.
